Formula 1 News

British GP 2025: Grandstand vs Paddock Club — Is the Upgrade Worth It?

ByOwen Castellano|10 February 2025  ·  6 min read

The British Grand Prix at Silverstone is one of the highlights of the Formula 1 calendar. (Getty Images)

We compare Silverstone's general admission, grandstand, and Paddock Club hospitality packages to help you decide what's worth your money.

Breaking Down Every Silverstone Ticket Category

Silverstone offers five main ticket tiers for the British Grand Prix. General Admission (from £85/day) grants access to the infield and designated standing areas but no fixed seat. Grandstand tickets (from £160/day) cover named stands including Copse, Maggotts, and the Pit Straight. Club Corner and Wing Hospitality suites (from £350/day) add catering and covered seating. The Formula 1 Paddock Club, the sport's premier hospitality product, starts at approximately £1,700 for a race-day pass and represents the highest tier available to the public.

Which Grandstand Gives the Best View of the Action?

The Hangar Straight grandstand offers one of the longest sustained views of any seat at Silverstone — you can watch cars accelerate from Stowe corner all the way down to Vale — and it remains one of the best-value named stands. The Pit Straight grandstand is iconic but largely obscured by the pit wall gantry unless you are in the upper tiers. For overtaking action, Club Corner and Brooklands are statistically the most active braking zones. If you plan to attend across the full weekend, buying a three-day grandstand pass saves an average of 22%.

What the Paddock Club Actually Includes (and What It Doesn't)

The Formula 1 Paddock Club hospitality is located directly above the pit lane and includes: a reserved seat in a glass-fronted suite, à la carte dining from a Michelin-trained kitchen, unlimited premium bar, a paddock walkabout pass, and opportunities to view garages during designated windows. What it does not include: a guarantee of driver meet-and-greets or pit lane access during sessions. For die-hard fans wanting the closest possible connection to the teams, it remains unmatched; for those primarily watching the race, a grandstand at one-tenth of the price delivers more raw racing drama.

How to Book Silverstone Tickets and Avoid the Resale Premium

Official Silverstone tickets for the British Grand Prix go on general sale in October each year. Demand for Race Day grandstands is particularly acute and they frequently sell out within hours. Setting up an account in advance and being logged in at the exact sale time is essential. For those who miss general sale, the official Silverstone Ticket Exchange allows fans to list unwanted tickets at face value — far safer than third-party platforms where premiums of 50–80% are common. If using a secondary platform, verify the listing includes mobile transfer delivery tied to your name.
